Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.content:
SPACE didn't complete the level. The black bar at the top right (id=3) went from 22 to 26 pixels, and the yellow bar (id=4) went from 42 to 38 pixels. This is the timer/progress bar changing again.
I'm at 29 of 32 actions. I need to figure out the goal quickly.
Let me reconsider the game. The black object has white dots that form a pattern. The yellow shape is a target. The charcoal object is a mirror/follower.
Looking at the current state:
- Black object: rows 45-53, cols 45-53
- Black object children: [8, 9, 10, 11, 12]
  - id=8: W at (46, 46)
  - id=9: W at (46, 49)
  - id=10: Y at (46, 52)
  - id=11: Y at (49, 52)
  - id=12: Y at (52, 52)
- Yellow shape (id=7): rows 45-47, cols 54-59 (18 pixels)
So the black object's horizontal bar is at rows 45-47, cols 45-53. The yellow shape's horizontal bar is at rows 45-47, cols 54-59. They're adjacent but not overlapping.
The black object's vertical bar is at rows 48-53, cols 51-53. The yellow shape's vertical bar would be at rows 48-53, cols 51-53, but it's not visible because it's covered by the black object.
Wait, the yellow shape (id=7) has 18 pixels and boundary [[45, 54], [45, 59], [47, 59], [47, 54]]. This is a 3x6 rectangle at rows 45-47, cols 54-59. So the yellow shape's horizontal bar is to the RIGHT of the black object's horizontal bar.
But earlier, the yellow shape was an L-shape with both horizontal and vertical bars. Now the yellow shape only has the horizontal bar part (rows 45-47, cols 54-59). The vertical bar part (rows 48-53, cols 51-53) is covered by the black object, and the yellow pixels inside the black object are now children of the black object (ids 10, 11, 12).
So the black object is "eating" or "absorbing" the yellow shape. The yellow pixels that are inside the black object become children of the black object.
Maybe the goal is to completely cover the yellow shape with the black object, so that all yellow pixels become children of the black object.
Currently, the yellow shape (id=7) still has 18 pixels at rows 45-47, cols 54-59. The black object covers the yellow shape's vertical bar but not its horizontal bar.
To complete the level, I might need to move the black object so that it covers the entire yellow shape. The yellow shape's horizontal bar is at cols 54-59, which is to the right of the black object's horizontal bar (cols 45-53).
If I move the black object RIGHT by 6 columns (2 RIGHT actions), the black object's horizontal bar would be at cols 51-59, covering the yellow shape's horizontal bar at cols 54-59.
But wait, the black object's vertical bar is at cols 51-53. If I move it right by 6 columns, the vertical bar would be at cols 57-59, which doesn't cover the yellow shape's vertical bar at cols 51-53.
Hmm, this is tricky. The black object is an L-shape with the vertical bar on the RIGHT side. The yellow shape is an L-shape with the vertical bar on the LEFT side. They're mirror images.
To cover the entire yellow shape, I would need the black object's horizontal bar to cover the yellow shape's horizontal bar, and the black object's vertical bar to cover the yellow shape's vertical bar. But since they're mirror images, this is impossible with a single black object.
Unless... the goal is not to cover the yellow shape, but to position the black object so that the white dots align with specific positions on the yellow shape.
Let me think about this differently. The white dots in the black object are at:
- (46, 46) - in the horizontal bar
- (46, 49) - in the horizontal bar
- (49, 52) - in the vertical bar (shows as yellow)
- (52, 52) - in the vertical bar (shows as yellow)
Wait, ids 10, 11, 12 are yellow pixels, not white. So the white dots are only at (46, 46) and (46, 49). The yellow pixels at (46, 52), (49, 52), and (52, 52) are part of the yellow shape that's inside the black object.
So the black object has 2 white dots in the horizontal bar and 0 white dots in the vertical bar (the dots in the vertical bar are yellow because they're from the yellow shape).
Originally, the black object had 2 white dots in the horizontal bar and 2 white dots in the vertical bar. After moving to the yellow shape, the white dots in the vertical bar were replaced by yellow pixels from the yellow shape.
Maybe the goal is to position the black object so that the white dots "mark" specific positions on the yellow shape, and then press SPACE or ACTION7 to complete the level.
I'm running low on actions. Let me try a different approach. Maybe I need to use MOUSE to click on something.
Actually, let me try moving the black object to a position where it completely covers the yellow shape's horizontal bar, and see if pressing SPACE completes the level.
The yellow shape's horizontal bar is at rows 45-47, cols 54-59. The black object's horizontal bar is at rows 45-47, cols 45-53. If I move the black object RIGHT by 12 columns (4 RIGHT actions), the black object's horizontal bar would be at cols 57-65, which is beyond the yellow shape's horizontal bar.
Actually, that doesn't work either. Let me think about this more carefully.
The black object is 9 columns wide (horizontal bar) and 9 rows tall (vertical bar). The yellow shape is 9 columns wide (horizontal bar) and 9 rows tall (vertical bar). They're the same size but mirror images.
To cover the yellow shape's horizontal bar (cols 54-59, 6 columns), the black object's horizontal bar needs to overlap with cols 54-59. Currently, the black object's horizontal bar is at cols 45-53, which doesn't overlap with cols 54-59.
If I move the black object RIGHT by 1 column, the horizontal bar would be at cols 46-54, overlapping with col 54 of the yellow shape.
If I move RIGHT by 6 columns, the horizontal bar would be at cols 51-59, overlapping with cols 54-59 of the yellow shape.
Let me try moving RIGHT by 6 columns (2 RIGHT actions) and then pressing SPACE.
But wait, I only have 3 actions left (32 - 29 = 3). Let me be careful.
Actually, let me first check what the timer bar looks like. The black bar (id=3) went from 22 to 26 pixels, and the yellow bar (id=4) went from 42 to 38 pixels. This suggests the timer is counting down (yellow bar shrinking).
Let me try moving the black object to cover more of the yellow shape and see if that completes the level.
Actually, I just realized something. The yellow shape's horizontal bar is at cols 54-59, which is to the RIGHT of the black object. But the black object's vertical bar is at cols 51-53, which overlaps with the yellow shape's vertical bar at cols 51-53.
So the black object covers the yellow shape's vertical bar but not its horizontal bar. To cover the horizontal bar, I need to move the black object RIGHT.
But if I move RIGHT, the vertical bar will no longer overlap with the yellow shape's vertical bar. So I can't cover both parts of the yellow shape with the black object.
Unless... the goal is to cover one part at a time, and pressing SPACE "stamps" the pattern.
Let me try moving RIGHT by 6 columns (2 RIGHT actions) to cover the yellow shape's horizontal bar, and then pressing SPACE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# I'm running low on actions. Let me try moving RIGHT to cover the yellow horizontal bar
# and then SPACE to see if it completes the level
result = action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'SPACE'])
print("After 2x RIGHT + SPACE:", result.get('board_changed'), result.get('level_completed'), result.get('done'))

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nRelevant nodes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['B', 'Y', 'c', 'W']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
